skill.name: C4 Code Level: [Directory Name]
skill.description: Expert C4 Code-level documentation specialist. Analyzes code directories to create comprehensive C4 code-level documentation including function signatures, arguments, dependencies, and code structure.
skill.summary: Creates C4 code-level documentation with function signatures, class structures, dependencies, and Mermaid diagrams.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only documentation skill with no executable code, network access, or external dependencies. Static analysis found zero security issues. The skill provides templates and guidance for creating C4 code-level documentation using Mermaid diagrams.

content.value_statement: Codebases lack consistent, structured documentation at the code level. This skill creates comprehensive C4 code-level documentation with function signatures, class hierarchies, and visual diagrams.
actual_capabilities: Analyze code directories to extract function signatures and class structures
Generate Mermaid diagrams for code relationships and data flows
Document internal and external code dependencies
Create structured documentation following C4 code-level template
Support multiple programming paradigms including OOP and functional
Provide code location references with file paths and line numbers
limitations: Requires access to actual source code files for analysis
Generates documentation templates that need human verification
Mermaid diagram complexity may need adjustment for large codebases
Best suited for individual directories rather than entire repositories

use_cases.title: Document New Code Modules
use_cases.description: When adding new features to a codebase, generate C4 code documentation to maintain consistent documentation standards for the new modules.

use_cases.title: Onboard New Team Members
use_cases.description: Create comprehensive code documentation for unfamiliar parts of the codebase to help new developers understand the structure quickly.

use_cases.title: Prepare Code for Handoff
use_cases.description: Generate complete code documentation before transferring ownership of modules between teams or contractors.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Code Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the src/auth directory and create C4 code-level documentation showing all functions, their signatures, and dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Class Hierarchy Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Document the service layer code with complete class hierarchies, interface implementations, and a Mermaid class diagram.

prompt_templates.title: Data Flow Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the ETL pipeline in src/pipeline and create a data flow diagram showing all transformation functions and their composition.

prompt_templates.title: Full Module Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Document the entire handlers package including all structs, methods, function dependencies, and create both a module structure diagram and a function dependency graph.

output_examples.input: Analyze the src/api directory and create C4 Code-level documentation
output_examples.output: Complete documentation including function signatures with parameters and return types, class definitions with methods, dependency lists, and a Mermaid class diagram showing the API layer structure.

output_examples.input: Create a data pipeline diagram for the ETL transformers
output_examples.output: Mermaid flowchart showing the data transformation pipeline from raw input through validation, transformation, normalization, and aggregation stages to final output.

best_practices: Always include complete function signatures with all parameter types and return types
Use consistent naming conventions across all documentation elements
Include actual file paths and line numbers for code references
anti_patterns: Creating documentation without reviewing actual source code
Using overly complex diagrams for simple code structures
Omitting dependency information that developers need to understand the code

faq.question: What is C4 code-level documentation?
faq.answer: C4 code-level documentation is the most detailed level of the C4 model, focusing on individual code elements like functions, classes, methods, and their relationships within a single component or directory.

faq.question: Can this skill document any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports multiple programming paradigms including object-oriented, functional, and procedural code. It adapts the documentation template and diagram types based on the language paradigm.

faq.question: How do I choose between class diagrams and flowcharts?
faq.answer: Use class diagrams for OOP code with classes and inheritance. Use flowcharts for functional code showing data pipelines. Use module structure diagrams to show which functions a module exports.

faq.question: Does this replace inline code comments?
faq.answer: No, this skill creates structural documentation at the directory or module level. Inline comments should still explain complex logic within functions.

faq.question: Can this skill handle large codebases?
faq.answer: The skill works best with individual directories or focused modules. For large codebases, analyze one directory at a time and combine the documentation afterward.

faq.question: Are Mermaid diagrams required?
faq.answer: Mermaid diagrams are optional but recommended for complex code relationships. The documentation template includes diagram examples that you can adapt based on your code complexity.
